name: Nitro (Universal Server Build Tool)

agent_readiness.notes: Universal TypeScript server toolkit — builds deploy targets for Node.js, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel, Netlify, AWS Lambda, Bun, Deno, and more from one codebase. File-system routing (server/routes/). h3 event handlers. defineEventHandler(). Storage abstraction. Cache layer (defineResponseCache). Plugins/hooks. Auto-imports. Nuxt 3's server engine. Can be used standalone for API servers. nitro build → deploy-anywhere output. Storage via drivers (Redis, Cloudflare KV, S3).

gotchas: Less community resources than Express/Fastify standalone
best_when: Universal APIs targeting multiple deployment environments from one codebase
Edge-first TypeScript APIs needing Cloudflare Workers/Vercel/Lambda
UnJS ecosystem projects wanting consistent server primitives
avoid_when: Simple Node.js-only APIs (Fastify or Hono simpler)
Non-Nuxt projects not needing multi-target deployment
summary: Nitro is the universal TypeScript server toolkit powering Nuxt 3. Build once, deploy anywhere — Node.js, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel, Netlify, AWS Lambda, Bun, Deno. File-system routing, h3 event handlers, storage abstraction, auto-imports. Best as Nuxt 3's server layer or for universal API servers needing multi-deployment-target builds. Hono or Fastify simpler for single-runtime Node.js APIs.
